methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $235M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Electra.aero is a Series B transportation-technology company founded by John Langford. The valuation analysis relies on a peer set of three private companies in the same sector and adjacent stages: Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M). No public comparables exist in the immediate comp universe.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression multiples, using total raised to date of $235M as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier for Series B advancement, yield an implied valuation of $446M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector comps produces a median valuation of $500M, with a range spanning $270M to $1.8B. The substantial spread reflects stage heterogeneity within the peer set and variation in company-specific circumstances.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $380M (low) to $575M (high), with a median estimate of $473M. This narrow band around $470M reflects broad consistency between stage-based and peer-relative approaches. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three-company peer set for a pre-revenue or early-revenue Stage B company, sensitivity to which specific peer multiples are deemed most comparable, and whether stage-progression multiples appropriately capture transportation-sector dynamics. The peer range of $270M to $1.8B underscores the wide dispersion of outcomes in frontier transportation ventures.
